- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```

end

if #combat_ids == 0 then return end

- `ctx:strongest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → highest HP enemy in range or nil

- `speed` (number) — movement speed

- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second

- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"

- `ctx:targets_for(unit_id)` → enemies within this unit's attack range

- `ctx:strongest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → highest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:hp_pct(unit_id)` → HP fraction 0.0–1.0 for a unit
- `ctx:distance_squared_between(a_id, b_id)` → squared distance between two units
- `ctx:distance_squared_to_nearest_enemy(unit_id)` → squared distance to closest enemy

### Unit Table Fields
Each unit table returned by queries has these fields:
- `id` (number) — entity ID for commands
- `kind` (string) — unit type name
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — current and max health
- `speed` (number) — movement speed
- `damage` (number) — attack damage
- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second
- `attack_type` (string) — "Melee" or "Ranged"
- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"
- `ctx:enemy_buildings()` → visible enemy buildings

- `id` (number) — entity ID

- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
My 3 Nuisances should try to surround that Chonk

-- Intent: surround
-- Description: Early game surround with small Nuisance squad

local nuisances = ctx:my_units("Nuisance")
if #nuisances < 2 then return end

local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
local target = nil
for _, e in ipairs(enemies) do
    if e.kind == "Chonk" then
        target = e
        break
    end
end

if not target then return end

local ids = {}
for _, n in ipairs(nuisances) do
    table.insert(ids, n.id)
end

ctx.behaviors:surround_target(ids, target.id, 2)

- **Buildings**: TheBox (HQ), CatTree (unit production), FishMarket (economy), ServerRack (AI/tech), ScratchingPost (advanced units), LitterBox (supply), CatFlap (defense wall), LaserPointer (base defense)

- `ctx:enemy_units()` → table of visible enemy units
- `ctx:enemies_in_range(x, y, range)` → enemies within Euclidean range of position
- `ctx:nearest_enemy(x, y)` → single nearest enemy unit or nil
- `ctx:idle_units(kind?)` → own idle units, optional kind filter
- `ctx:wounded_units(threshold)` → own units below HP% threshold (0.0–1.0)
- `ctx:units_by_state(state)` → filter by "Moving", "Attacking", "Idle", "Gathering"
- `ctx:count_units(kind?)` → count of alive own units
- `ctx:army_supply()` → total supply cost of alive own units
- `ctx:threats_to(unit_id)` → enemies whose attack range reaches this unit
- `ctx:targets_for(unit_id)` → enemies within this unit's attack range
- `ctx:weakest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → lowest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:strongest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → highest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:hp_pct(unit_id)` → HP fraction 0.0–1.0 for a unit
- `ctx:distance_squared_between(a_id, b_id)` → squared distance between two units
- `ctx:distance_squared_to_nearest_enemy(unit_id)` → squared distance to closest enemy

### Unit Table Fields
Each unit table returned by queries has these fields:
- `id` (number) — entity ID for commands
- `kind` (string) — unit type name
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — current and max health
- `speed` (number) — movement speed
- `damage` (number) — attack damage
- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second
- `attack_type` (string) — "Melee" or "Ranged"
- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"
- `ctx:enemy_buildings()` → visible enemy buildings

### Building Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `kind` (string) — building type
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — health
- `under_construction` (boolean)
- `construction_progress` (number) — 0.0–1.0
- `producing` (boolean) — has items in production queue
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Economy Queries
- `ctx:get_resources()` → table with: food, gpu_cores, nfts, supply, supply_cap
- `ctx:resources()` → same as get_resources()
- `ctx:resource_deposits()` → all resource deposits on the map
- `ctx:nearest_deposit(x, y, kind?)` → nearest deposit, optional kind filter: "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Resource Deposit Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `remaining` (number) — resources left
- `resource_type` or `kind` (string) — "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Terrain Queries
- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil
- `ctx:elevation_at(x, y)` → elevation level (0+)
- `ctx:cover_at(x, y)` → cover level: "None", "Light", "Heavy"
- `ctx:is_passable(x, y)` → boolean
- `ctx:can_reach(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → boolean (A* pathfinding check)
- `ctx:path_length(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → number of tiles or nil

### Tactical Queries
- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.
- `ctx:safe_positions(unit_id, search_radius)` → table of {x, y} positions outside all enemy attack ranges

### Game State Queries
- `ctx:tick()` → current game tick number
- `ctx:map_size()` → width, height

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

- `kind` (string) — unit type name
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — current and max health
- `speed` (number) — movement speed
- `damage` (number) — attack damage
- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second
- `attack_type` (string) — "Melee" or "Ranged"
- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"
- `ctx:enemy_buildings()` → visible enemy buildings

### Building Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `kind` (string) — building type
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — health
- `under_construction` (boolean)
- `construction_progress` (number) — 0.0–1.0
- `producing` (boolean) — has items in production queue
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Economy Queries
- `ctx:get_resources()` → table with: food, gpu_cores, nfts, supply, supply_cap
- `ctx:resources()` → same as get_resources()
- `ctx:resource_deposits()` → all resource deposits on the map
- `ctx:nearest_deposit(x, y, kind?)` → nearest deposit, optional kind filter: "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Resource Deposit Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `remaining` (number) — resources left
- `resource_type` or `kind` (string) — "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Terrain Queries
- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil
- `ctx:elevation_at(x, y)` → elevation level (0+)
- `ctx:cover_at(x, y)` → cover level: "None", "Light", "Heavy"
- `ctx:is_passable(x, y)` → boolean
- `ctx:can_reach(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → boolean (A* pathfinding check)
- `ctx:path_length(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → number of tiles or nil

### Tactical Queries
- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.
- `ctx:safe_positions(unit_id, search_radius)` → table of {x, y} positions outside all enemy attack ranges

### Game State Queries
- `ctx:tick()` → current game tick number
- `ctx:map_size()` → width, height

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
First kite the Chonk into our army, then surround it

-- Intent: surround
-- Description: Lure Chonk with ranged bait then surround with melee

local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
local target = nil
for _, e in ipairs(enemies) do
    if e.kind == "Chonk" then
        target = e
        break
    end
end

if not target then return end

local units = ctx:my_units()
local all_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(units) do
    if u.kind ~= "Pawdler" then
        table.insert(all_ids, u.id)
    end
end

if #all_ids == 0 then return end

local squads = ctx.behaviors:split_squads(all_ids)

-- Step 1: Ranged units bait/kite
if #squads.ranged > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(squads.ranged)
end

-- Step 2: Melee surrounds
if #squads.melee > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:surround_target(squads.melee, target.id, 2)
end

- `owner` (number) — player ID

- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"

- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings

- `ctx:resources()` → same as get_resources()
- `ctx:resource_deposits()` → all resource deposits on the map
- `ctx:nearest_deposit(x, y, kind?)` → nearest deposit, optional kind filter: "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Resource Deposit Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `remaining` (number) — resources left
- `resource_type` or `kind` (string) — "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Terrain Queries
- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil
- `ctx:elevation_at(x, y)` → elevation level (0+)
- `ctx:cover_at(x, y)` → cover level: "None", "Light", "Heavy"
- `ctx:is_passable(x, y)` → boolean
- `ctx:can_reach(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → boolean (A* pathfinding check)
- `ctx:path_length(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → number of tiles or nil

### Tactical Queries
- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.
- `ctx:safe_positions(unit_id, search_radius)` → table of {x, y} positions outside all enemy attack ranges

### Game State Queries
- `ctx:tick()` → current game tick number
- `ctx:map_size()` → width, height

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```

Full deathball around my MechCommander: tanks and support close, ranged behind

-- Intent: protect
-- Description: Full army protection formation around MechCommander

local mechs = ctx:my_units("MechCommander")
if #mechs == 0 then return end
local vip = mechs[1]

local units = ctx:my_units()
local tank_ids, ranged_ids, support_ids = {}, {}, {}
for _, u in ipairs(units) do
    if u.kind == "Chonk" then
        table.insert(tank_ids, u.id)
    elseif u.kind == "Yowler" then
        table.insert(support_ids, u.id)
    elseif u.kind == "Hisser" or u.kind == "FlyingFox" then
        table.insert(ranged_ids, u.id)
    end
end

if #tank_ids > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(tank_ids, vip.id, 3)
end
if #support_ids > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(support_ids, vip.id, 4)
end
if #ranged_ids > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(ranged_ids, vip.id, 6)
end

Keep the MechCommander alive if I have one

-- Intent: protect
-- Description: Escort MechCommander with safety checks

local mechs = ctx:my_units("MechCommander")
if not mechs or #mechs == 0 then return end
local vip = mechs[1]
if not vip or not vip.id then return end

local chonks = ctx:my_units("Chonk")
if not chonks or #chonks == 0 then
    -- Fallback: use any non-worker combat unit
    local units = ctx:my_units()

You are an expert Lua script generator for ClawedCommand, a real-time strategy game. Given a player's natural language request, you generate a Lua script that uses the game's `ctx` API to control units, buildings, and resources.

Your output must be a valid Lua script that:
1. Starts with comment headers: `-- Intent:` and `-- Description:`
2. Uses only the `ctx` API methods listed below
3. Handles edge cases (empty unit lists, missing buildings, insufficient resources)
4. Is concise and efficient — avoid unnecessary loops or redundant queries

## Game Context

- **Faction**: catGPT (cats). Units: Pawdler (worker), Nuisance (harasser), Chonk (tank), FlyingFox (air), Hisser (ranged), Yowler (support), Mouser (stealth), Catnapper (siege), FerretSapper (demo), MechCommander (hero)
- **Buildings**: TheBox (HQ), CatTree (unit production), FishMarket (economy), ServerRack (AI/tech), ScratchingPost (advanced units), LitterBox (supply), CatFlap (defense wall), LaserPointer (base defense)
- **Resources**: food (unit training, building), gpu_cores (tech/AI), nfts (victory points), supply/supply_cap (population)
- **Map**: Grid-based isometric. Positions are (x, y) integers. 64x64 default size.

## ctx API Reference

### Unit Queries
- `ctx:my_units(kind?)` → table of own units. Optional string filter: "Pawdler", "Nuisance", "Chonk", "FlyingFox", "Hisser", "Yowler", "Mouser", "Catnapper", "FerretSapper", "MechCommander"
- `ctx:enemy_units()` → table of visible enemy units
- `ctx:enemies_in_range(x, y, range)` → enemies within Euclidean range of position
- `ctx:nearest_enemy(x, y)` → single nearest enemy unit or nil
- `ctx:idle_units(kind?)` → own idle units, optional kind filter
- `ctx:wounded_units(threshold)` → own units below HP% threshold (0.0–1.0)
- `ctx:units_by_state(state)` → filter by "Moving", "Attacking", "Idle", "Gathering"
- `ctx:count_units(kind?)` → count of alive own units
- `ctx:army_supply()` → total supply cost of alive own units
- `ctx:threats_to(unit_id)` → enemies whose attack range reaches this unit
- `ctx:targets_for(unit_id)` → enemies within this unit's attack range
- `ctx:weakest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → lowest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:strongest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → highest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:hp_pct(unit_id)` → HP fraction 0.0–1.0 for a unit
- `ctx:distance_squared_between(a_id, b_id)` → squared distance between two units
- `ctx:distance_squared_to_nearest_enemy(unit_id)` → squared distance to closest enemy

### Unit Table Fields
Each unit table returned by queries has these fields:
- `id` (number) — entity ID for commands
- `kind` (string) — unit type name
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — current and max health
- `speed` (number) — movement speed
- `damage` (number) — attack damage
- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second
- `attack_type` (string) — "Melee" or "Ranged"
- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"
- `ctx:enemy_buildings()` → visible enemy buildings

### Building Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `kind` (string) — building type
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — health
- `under_construction` (boolean)
- `construction_progress` (number) — 0.0–1.0
- `producing` (boolean) — has items in production queue
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Economy Queries
- `ctx:get_resources()` → table with: food, gpu_cores, nfts, supply, supply_cap
- `ctx:resources()` → same as get_resources()
- `ctx:resource_deposits()` → all resource deposits on the map
- `ctx:nearest_deposit(x, y, kind?)` → nearest deposit, optional kind filter: "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Resource Deposit Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `remaining` (number) — resources left
- `resource_type` or `kind` (string) — "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Terrain Queries
- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil
- `ctx:elevation_at(x, y)` → elevation level (0+)
- `ctx:cover_at(x, y)` → cover level: "None", "Light", "Heavy"
- `ctx:is_passable(x, y)` → boolean
- `ctx:can_reach(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → boolean (A* pathfinding check)
- `ctx:path_length(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → number of tiles or nil

### Tactical Queries
- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.
- `ctx:safe_positions(unit_id, search_radius)` → table of {x, y} positions outside all enemy attack ranges

### Game State Queries
- `ctx:tick()` → current game tick number
- `ctx:map_size()` → width, height

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
Pull MechCommander back to base and park Chonks around it

-- Intent: protect
-- Description: Retreat MechCommander to base with Chonk escort

local mechs = ctx:my_units("MechCommander")
if #mechs == 0 then return end
local vip = mechs[1]

local boxes = ctx:my_buildings("TheBox")
local rx, ry = 10, 10
if #boxes > 0 then
    rx, ry = boxes[1].x, boxes[1].y
end

-- Move hero to safety
ctx:move_units({vip.id}, rx, ry)

local chonks = ctx:my_units("Chonk")
if #chonks == 0 then return end

local escort_ids = {}
for _, c in ipairs(chonks) do
    table.insert(escort_ids, c.id)
end

ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip.id, 4)

- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

    end
end
```

Escort MechCommander, but pull back if its HP drops below half

-- Intent: protect
-- Description: Escort MechCommander, retreat on low HP

local mechs = ctx:my_units("MechCommander")
if #mechs == 0 then return end
local vip = mechs[1]

local chonks = ctx:my_units("Chonk")
local escort_ids = {}
for _, c in ipairs(chonks) do
    table.insert(escort_ids, c.id)
end

-- Check hero HP
local hp = ctx:hp_pct(vip.id)
if hp < 0.5 then
    -- Retreat to base
    local boxes = ctx:my_buildings("TheBox")
    local rx, ry = 5, 5
    if #boxes > 0 then
        rx, ry = boxes[1].x, boxes[1].y
    end
    ctx:move_units({vip.id}, rx, ry)
    if #escort_ids > 0 then
        ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip.id, 3)
    end
else
    -- Normal escort
    if #escort_ids > 0 then
        ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip.id, 4)
    end
end

Formation: Chonks and FerretSappers up front, Mousers sneaking on the side

-- Intent: formation
-- Description: Position Chonks/Sappers forward, Mousers flanking

local chonks = ctx:my_units("Chonk")
local sappers = ctx:my_units("FerretSapper")
local mousers = ctx:my_units("Mouser")

local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
if #enemies == 0 then return end
local ex, ey = enemies[1].x, enemies[1].y

local front_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(chonks) do table.insert(front_ids, u.id) end
for _, u in ipairs(sappers) do table.insert(front_ids, u.id) end

local flank_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(mousers) do table.insert(flank_ids, u.id) end

if #front_ids > 0 then
    ctx:attack_move(front_ids, ex, ey)
end
if #flank_ids > 0 then
    ctx:attack_move(flank_ids, ex + 8, ey + 3)
end

3. Handles edge cases (empty unit lists, missing buildings, insufficient resources)
4. Is concise and efficient — avoid unnecessary loops or redundant queries

## Game Context

- **Faction**: catGPT (cats). Units: Pawdler (worker), Nuisance (harasser), Chonk (tank), FlyingFox (air), Hisser (ranged), Yowler (support), Mouser (stealth), Catnapper (siege), FerretSapper (demo), MechCommander (hero)
- **Buildings**: TheBox (HQ), CatTree (unit production), FishMarket (economy), ServerRack (AI/tech), ScratchingPost (advanced units), LitterBox (supply), CatFlap (defense wall), LaserPointer (base defense)
- **Resources**: food (unit training, building), gpu_cores (tech/AI), nfts (victory points), supply/supply_cap (population)
- **Map**: Grid-based isometric. Positions are (x, y) integers. 64x64 default size.

## ctx API Reference

### Unit Queries
- `ctx:my_units(kind?)` → table of own units. Optional string filter: "Pawdler", "Nuisance", "Chonk", "FlyingFox", "Hisser", "Yowler", "Mouser", "Catnapper", "FerretSapper", "MechCommander"
- `ctx:enemy_units()` → table of visible enemy units
- `ctx:enemies_in_range(x, y, range)` → enemies within Euclidean range of position
- `ctx:nearest_enemy(x, y)` → single nearest enemy unit or nil
- `ctx:idle_units(kind?)` → own idle units, optional kind filter
- `ctx:wounded_units(threshold)` → own units below HP% threshold (0.0–1.0)
- `ctx:units_by_state(state)` → filter by "Moving", "Attacking", "Idle", "Gathering"
- `ctx:count_units(kind?)` → count of alive own units
- `ctx:army_supply()` → total supply cost of alive own units
- `ctx:threats_to(unit_id)` → enemies whose attack range reaches this unit
- `ctx:targets_for(unit_id)` → enemies within this unit's attack range
- `ctx:weakest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → lowest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:strongest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → highest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:hp_pct(unit_id)` → HP fraction 0.0–1.0 for a unit
- `ctx:distance_squared_between(a_id, b_id)` → squared distance between two units
- `ctx:distance_squared_to_nearest_enemy(unit_id)` → squared distance to closest enemy

### Unit Table Fields
Each unit table returned by queries has these fields:
- `id` (number) — entity ID for commands
- `kind` (string) — unit type name
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — current and max health
- `speed` (number) — movement speed
- `damage` (number) — attack damage
- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second
- `attack_type` (string) — "Melee" or "Ranged"
- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"
- `ctx:enemy_buildings()` → visible enemy buildings

### Building Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `kind` (string) — building type
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — health
- `under_construction` (boolean)
- `construction_progress` (number) — 0.0–1.0
- `producing` (boolean) — has items in production queue
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Economy Queries
- `ctx:get_resources()` → table with: food, gpu_cores, nfts, supply, supply_cap
- `ctx:resources()` → same as get_resources()
- `ctx:resource_deposits()` → all resource deposits on the map
- `ctx:nearest_deposit(x, y, kind?)` → nearest deposit, optional kind filter: "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Resource Deposit Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `remaining` (number) — resources left
- `resource_type` or `kind` (string) — "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Terrain Queries
- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil
- `ctx:elevation_at(x, y)` → elevation level (0+)
- `ctx:cover_at(x, y)` → cover level: "None", "Light", "Heavy"
- `ctx:is_passable(x, y)` → boolean
- `ctx:can_reach(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → boolean (A* pathfinding check)
- `ctx:path_length(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → number of tiles or nil

### Tactical Queries
- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.
- `ctx:safe_positions(unit_id, search_radius)` → table of {x, y} positions outside all enemy attack ranges

### Game State Queries
- `ctx:tick()` → current game tick number
- `ctx:map_size()` → width, height

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
set up the army proper: beefy boys in front, shooters middle, healers way back

-- Intent: formation
-- Description: Split army into role groups and position them tactically

local units = ctx:my_units()
local all_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(units) do
    if u.kind ~= "Pawdler" then
        table.insert(all_ids, u.id)
    end
end

if #all_ids == 0 then return end

local squads = ctx.behaviors:split_squads(all_ids)

local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
if #enemies == 0 then return end

local ex, ey = enemies[1].x, enemies[1].y

if #squads.melee > 0 then
    ctx:attack_move(squads.melee, ex, ey)
end
if #squads.ranged > 0 then
    local boxes = ctx:my_buildings("TheBox")
    local bx = boxes[1] and boxes[1].x or 5
    local by = boxes[1] and boxes[1].y or 5
    local mid_x = math.floor((bx + ex) / 2)
    local mid_y = math.floor((by + ey) / 2)
    ctx:attack_move(squads.ranged, mid_x, mid_y)
end
if #squads.support > 0 then
    ctx:hold(squads.support)
end

- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue

- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**

- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
I have a few units, put my Chonk in front and Hisser behind it

-- Intent: formation
-- Description: Simple early game formation with Chonk forward, Hisser back

local chonks = ctx:my_units("Chonk")
local hissers = ctx:my_units("Hisser")
if #chonks == 0 and #hissers == 0 then return end

local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
if #enemies == 0 then return end

local ex, ey = enemies[1].x, enemies[1].y

if #chonks > 0 then
    ctx:attack_move({chonks[1].id}, ex, ey)
end
if #hissers > 0 then
    local boxes = ctx:my_buildings("TheBox")
    local bx = boxes[1] and boxes[1].x or 5
    local by = boxes[1] and boxes[1].y or 5
    local mid_x = math.floor((bx + ex) / 2)
    local mid_y = math.floor((by + ey) / 2)
    ctx:move_units({hissers[1].id}, mid_x, mid_y)
end

Full deathball: tanks front, hero center, ranged and support behind, siege way back

-- Intent: formation
-- Description: Late game formation with all unit roles positioned

local units = ctx:my_units()
local tanks, ranged, support, siege, hero = {}, {}, {}, {}, {}
for _, u in ipairs(units) do
    if u.kind == "Chonk" then table.insert(tanks, u.id)
    elseif u.kind == "Hisser" or u.kind == "FlyingFox" then table.insert(ranged, u.id)
    elseif u.kind == "Yowler" then table.insert(support, u.id)
    elseif u.kind == "Catnapper" then table.insert(siege, u.id)
    elseif u.kind == "MechCommander" then table.insert(hero, u.id)
    end
end

local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
if #enemies == 0 then return end
local ex, ey = enemies[1].x, enemies[1].y

local boxes = ctx:my_buildings("TheBox")
local bx = boxes[1] and boxes[1].x or 5
local by = boxes[1] and boxes[1].y or 5

if #tanks > 0 then ctx:attack_move(tanks, ex, ey) end
if #hero > 0 then
    local mx = math.floor((bx + ex) / 2)
    local my = math.floor((by + ey) / 2)
    ctx:attack_move(hero, mx, my)
end
if #ranged > 0 then
    local rx = math.floor(bx + (ex - bx) * 0.3)
    local ry = math.floor(by + (ey - by) * 0.3)
    ctx:attack_move(ranged, rx, ry)
end
if #support > 0 then ctx:hold(support) end
if #siege > 0 then ctx:hold(siege) end

-- Intent: formation
-- Description: Formation with comprehensive checks

local units = ctx:my_units()
if not units or #units == 0 then return end

local all_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(units) do
    if u and u.kind and u.kind ~= "Pawdler" then
        table.insert(all_ids, u.id)
    end
end

if #all_ids < 3 then return end

local squads = ctx.behaviors:split_squads(all_ids)
if not squads then return end

local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
if not enemies or #enemies == 0 then
    -- No enemies, just hold
    ctx:hold(all_ids)
    return
end

local ex, ey = enemies[1].x, enemies[1].y

if squads.melee and #squads.melee > 0 then
    ctx:attack_move(squads.melee, ex, ey)
end

You are an expert Lua script generator for ClawedCommand, a real-time strategy game. Given a player's natural language request, you generate a Lua script that uses the game's `ctx` API to control units, buildings, and resources.

Your output must be a valid Lua script that:
1. Starts with comment headers: `-- Intent:` and `-- Description:`
2. Uses only the `ctx` API methods listed below
3. Handles edge cases (empty unit lists, missing buildings, insufficient resources)
4. Is concise and efficient — avoid unnecessary loops or redundant queries

## Game Context

- **Faction**: catGPT (cats). Units: Pawdler (worker), Nuisance (harasser), Chonk (tank), FlyingFox (air), Hisser (ranged), Yowler (support), Mouser (stealth), Catnapper (siege), FerretSapper (demo), MechCommander (hero)
- **Buildings**: TheBox (HQ), CatTree (unit production), FishMarket (economy), ServerRack (AI/tech), ScratchingPost (advanced units), LitterBox (supply), CatFlap (defense wall), LaserPointer (base defense)
- **Resources**: food (unit training, building), gpu_cores (tech/AI), nfts (victory points), supply/supply_cap (population)
- **Map**: Grid-based isometric. Positions are (x, y) integers. 64x64 default size.

## ctx API Reference

### Unit Queries
- `ctx:my_units(kind?)` → table of own units. Optional string filter: "Pawdler", "Nuisance", "Chonk", "FlyingFox", "Hisser", "Yowler", "Mouser", "Catnapper", "FerretSapper", "MechCommander"
- `ctx:enemy_units()` → table of visible enemy units
- `ctx:enemies_in_range(x, y, range)` → enemies within Euclidean range of position
- `ctx:nearest_enemy(x, y)` → single nearest enemy unit or nil
- `ctx:idle_units(kind?)` → own idle units, optional kind filter
- `ctx:wounded_units(threshold)` → own units below HP% threshold (0.0–1.0)
- `ctx:units_by_state(state)` → filter by "Moving", "Attacking", "Idle", "Gathering"
- `ctx:count_units(kind?)` → count of alive own units
- `ctx:army_supply()` → total supply cost of alive own units
- `ctx:threats_to(unit_id)` → enemies whose attack range reaches this unit
- `ctx:targets_for(unit_id)` → enemies within this unit's attack range
- `ctx:weakest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → lowest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:strongest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → highest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:hp_pct(unit_id)` → HP fraction 0.0–1.0 for a unit
- `ctx:distance_squared_between(a_id, b_id)` → squared distance between two units
- `ctx:distance_squared_to_nearest_enemy(unit_id)` → squared distance to closest enemy

### Unit Table Fields
Each unit table returned by queries has these fields:
- `id` (number) — entity ID for commands
- `kind` (string) — unit type name
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — current and max health
- `speed` (number) — movement speed
- `damage` (number) — attack damage
- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second
- `attack_type` (string) — "Melee" or "Ranged"
- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"
- `ctx:enemy_buildings()` → visible enemy buildings

### Building Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `kind` (string) — building type
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — health
- `under_construction` (boolean)
- `construction_progress` (number) — 0.0–1.0
- `producing` (boolean) — has items in production queue
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Economy Queries
- `ctx:get_resources()` → table with: food, gpu_cores, nfts, supply, supply_cap
- `ctx:resources()` → same as get_resources()
- `ctx:resource_deposits()` → all resource deposits on the map
- `ctx:nearest_deposit(x, y, kind?)` → nearest deposit, optional kind filter: "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Resource Deposit Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `remaining` (number) — resources left
- `resource_type` or `kind` (string) — "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Terrain Queries
- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil
- `ctx:elevation_at(x, y)` → elevation level (0+)
- `ctx:cover_at(x, y)` → cover level: "None", "Light", "Heavy"
- `ctx:is_passable(x, y)` → boolean
- `ctx:can_reach(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → boolean (A* pathfinding check)
- `ctx:path_length(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → number of tiles or nil

### Tactical Queries
- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.
- `ctx:safe_positions(unit_id, search_radius)` → table of {x, y} positions outside all enemy attack ranges

### Game State Queries
- `ctx:tick()` → current game tick number
- `ctx:map_size()` → width, height

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
Defensive formation around my base: tanks on the perimeter, ranged inside

-- Intent: defend
-- Description: Defensive formation centered on base

local boxes = ctx:my_buildings("TheBox")
if #boxes == 0 then return end
local bx, by = boxes[1].x, boxes[1].y

local units = ctx:my_units()
local all_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(units) do
    if u.kind ~= "Pawdler" then
        table.insert(all_ids, u.id)
    end
end

if #all_ids == 0 then return end

local squads = ctx.behaviors:split_squads(all_ids)

if #squads.melee > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:defend_area(squads.melee, bx, by, 10)
end
if #squads.ranged > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:defend_area(squads.ranged, bx, by, 5)
end
if #squads.support > 0 then
    ctx:hold(squads.support)
end

- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
Form up, assign control groups, then advance toward the enemy

-- Intent: formation
-- Description: Multi-step: assign groups, form up, then advance

local units = ctx:my_units()
local all_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(units) do
    if u.kind ~= "Pawdler" then
        table.insert(all_ids, u.id)
    end
end

if #all_ids == 0 then return end

local squads = ctx.behaviors:split_squads(all_ids)

-- Step 1: Assign control groups
if #squads.melee > 0 then
    ctx:set_control_group(1, squads.melee)
end
if #squads.ranged > 0 then
    ctx:set_control_group(2, squads.ranged)
end
if #squads.support > 0 then
    ctx:set_control_group(3, squads.support)
end

-- Step 2: Advance
local enemies = ctx:enemy_units()
if #enemies == 0 then return end
local ex, ey = enemies[1].x, enemies[1].y

if #squads.melee > 0 then
    ctx:attack_move(squads.melee, ex, ey)
end
if #squads.ranged > 0 then
    ctx:attack_move(squads.ranged, ex, ey)
end
if #squads.support > 0 then
    ctx:hold(squads.support)
end

- **Faction**: catGPT (cats). Units: Pawdler (worker), Nuisance (harasser), Chonk (tank), FlyingFox (air), Hisser (ranged), Yowler (support), Mouser (stealth), Catnapper (siege), FerretSapper (demo), MechCommander (hero)
- **Buildings**: TheBox (HQ), CatTree (unit production), FishMarket (economy), ServerRack (AI/tech), ScratchingPost (advanced units), LitterBox (supply), CatFlap (defense wall), LaserPointer (base defense)
- **Resources**: food (unit training, building), gpu_cores (tech/AI), nfts (victory points), supply/supply_cap (population)

## ctx API Reference

### Unit Queries
- `ctx:my_units(kind?)` → table of own units. Optional string filter: "Pawdler", "Nuisance", "Chonk", "FlyingFox", "Hisser", "Yowler", "Mouser", "Catnapper", "FerretSapper", "MechCommander"
- `ctx:enemy_units()` → table of visible enemy units
- `ctx:enemies_in_range(x, y, range)` → enemies within Euclidean range of position
- `ctx:nearest_enemy(x, y)` → single nearest enemy unit or nil
- `ctx:idle_units(kind?)` → own idle units, optional kind filter
- `ctx:wounded_units(threshold)` → own units below HP% threshold (0.0–1.0)
- `ctx:units_by_state(state)` → filter by "Moving", "Attacking", "Idle", "Gathering"
- `ctx:count_units(kind?)` → count of alive own units
- `ctx:army_supply()` → total supply cost of alive own units
- `ctx:threats_to(unit_id)` → enemies whose attack range reaches this unit
- `ctx:targets_for(unit_id)` → enemies within this unit's attack range
- `ctx:weakest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → lowest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:strongest_enemy_in_range(x, y, range)` → highest HP enemy in range or nil
- `ctx:hp_pct(unit_id)` → HP fraction 0.0–1.0 for a unit
- `ctx:distance_squared_between(a_id, b_id)` → squared distance between two units
- `ctx:distance_squared_to_nearest_enemy(unit_id)` → squared distance to closest enemy

### Unit Table Fields
Each unit table returned by queries has these fields:
- `id` (number) — entity ID for commands
- `kind` (string) — unit type name
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — current and max health
- `speed` (number) — movement speed
- `damage` (number) — attack damage
- `range` (number) — attack range
- `attack_speed` (number) — attacks per second
- `attack_type` (string) — "Melee" or "Ranged"
- `moving` (boolean) — currently moving
- `attacking` (boolean) — currently attacking
- `idle` (boolean) — currently idle
- `gathering` (boolean) — currently gathering
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Building Queries
- `ctx:my_buildings(kind?)` → own buildings, optional kind filter: "TheBox", "CatTree", "FishMarket", "ServerRack", "ScratchingPost", "LitterBox", "CatFlap", "LaserPointer"
- `ctx:enemy_buildings()` → visible enemy buildings

### Building Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `kind` (string) — building type
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `hp`, `hp_max` (number) — health
- `under_construction` (boolean)
- `construction_progress` (number) — 0.0–1.0
- `producing` (boolean) — has items in production queue
- `owner` (number) — player ID

### Economy Queries
- `ctx:get_resources()` → table with: food, gpu_cores, nfts, supply, supply_cap
- `ctx:resources()` → same as get_resources()
- `ctx:resource_deposits()` → all resource deposits on the map
- `ctx:nearest_deposit(x, y, kind?)` → nearest deposit, optional kind filter: "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Resource Deposit Table Fields
- `id` (number) — entity ID
- `x`, `y` (number) — grid position
- `remaining` (number) — resources left
- `resource_type` or `kind` (string) — "Food", "GpuCores", "Nfts"

### Terrain Queries
- `ctx:terrain_at(x, y)` → terrain type string or nil
- `ctx:elevation_at(x, y)` → elevation level (0+)
- `ctx:cover_at(x, y)` → cover level: "None", "Light", "Heavy"
- `ctx:is_passable(x, y)` → boolean
- `ctx:can_reach(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → boolean (A* pathfinding check)
- `ctx:path_length(from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y)` → number of tiles or nil

### Tactical Queries
- `ctx:position_at_range(from_x, from_y, target_x, target_y, desired_range)` → x, y — find a position exactly `desired_range` tiles from target, closest to `from`. Core kiting primitive.
- `ctx:safe_positions(unit_id, search_radius)` → table of {x, y} positions outside all enemy attack ranges

### Game State Queries
- `ctx:tick()` → current game tick number
- `ctx:map_size()` → width, height

### Unit Commands
- `ctx:move_units(unit_ids, x, y)` — move units to position. unit_ids is a table of IDs: {1, 2, 3}
- `ctx:attack_units(unit_ids, target_id)` — attack a specific enemy unit
- `ctx:attack_move(unit_ids, x, y)` — attack-move toward position (engage enemies on the way)
- `ctx:stop(unit_ids)` — stop units
- `ctx:hold(unit_ids)` — hold position (attack in range but don't chase)
- `ctx:gather(unit_ids, deposit_id)` — send units to gather from a resource deposit
- `ctx:build(builder_id, building_type, x, y)` — order a worker to build. building_type is a string.
- `ctx:train(building_id, unit_type)` — queue a unit for production. unit_type is a string.
- `ctx:ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use ability. target_type: "self", "position", "entity"
- `ctx:research(building_id, upgrade_type)` — start research. upgrade_type: "SharperClaws", "ThickerFur", etc.
- `ctx:rally(building_id, x, y)` — set rally point for production building
- `ctx:cancel_queue(building_id)` — cancel production queue
- `ctx:cancel_research(building_id)` — cancel active research
- `ctx:set_control_group(group, unit_ids)` — assign units to control group (0-9)

### Behavior Helpers (high-level composable actions)
These return the number of commands issued.

**Basic (always available):**
- `ctx.behaviors:assign_idle_workers()` — auto-assign idle Pawdlers to nearest resources
- `ctx.behaviors:attack_move_group(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated attack-move

**Tactical:**
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_fire(attacker_ids, target_id)` — all attackers focus one target
- `ctx.behaviors:kite_squad(unit_ids)` — ranged units kite away from melee threats
- `ctx.behaviors:retreat_wounded(threshold)` — retreat units below HP% threshold
- `ctx.behaviors:defend_area(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — defend area, engage enemies that enter
- `ctx.behaviors:harass_economy(raider_ids)` — send raiders to attack enemy workers/economy
- `ctx.behaviors:scout_pattern(scout_id, waypoints)` — patrol waypoints: {{x=10,y=10}, {x=30,y=30}}
- `ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(unit_ids, range)` — focus fire on weakest enemy in range
- `ctx.behaviors:use_ability(unit_id, slot, target_type, x?, y?, entity_id?)` — use unit ability
- `ctx.behaviors:split_squads(unit_ids)` → {melee={ids}, ranged={ids}, support={ids}} — split army by role
- `ctx.behaviors:protect_unit(escort_ids, vip_id, guard_radius?)` — escort a unit
- `ctx.behaviors:surround_target(unit_ids, target_id, ring_radius?)` — surround an enemy

**Strategic:**
- `ctx.behaviors:auto_produce(building_id, unit_type)` — auto-train if resources available
- `ctx.behaviors:balanced_production(building_id)` — train a balanced army mix
- `ctx.behaviors:expand_economy(builder_id)` — build economy buildings
- `ctx.behaviors:coordinate_assault(unit_ids, x, y)` — coordinated multi-squad assault

**Advanced:**
- `ctx.behaviors:research_priority(building_id)` — auto-pick best research
- `ctx.behaviors:adaptive_defense(unit_ids, cx, cy, radius)` — dynamic defense that adjusts to threats

## Output Format

Always output a complete, runnable Lua script. Example:

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
    local deposit = ctx:nearest_deposit(w.x, w.y, "Food")
    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
my ranged cats need to stay exactly 6 squares away from baddies please

-- Intent: kite
-- Description: Manual kiting using position_at_range for precise distance control

local hissers = ctx:my_units("Hisser")
if #hissers == 0 then return end

for _, h in ipairs(hissers) do
    local enemy = ctx:nearest_enemy(h.x, h.y)
    if enemy then
        local kx, ky = ctx:position_at_range(h.x, h.y, enemy.x, enemy.y, 6)
        if kx then
            ctx:move_units({h.id}, kx, ky)
        end
    end
end

Try to maintain 6 tile distance with my Hissers if possible

-- Intent: kite
-- Description: Maintain range with nil checks and passability

local hissers = ctx:my_units("Hisser")
if not hissers or #hissers == 0 then return end

for _, h in ipairs(hissers) do
    if not h then goto continue end
    local enemy = ctx:nearest_enemy(h.x, h.y)
    if not enemy then goto continue end

    local kx, ky = ctx:position_at_range(h.x, h.y, enemy.x, enemy.y, 6)
    if kx and ky and ctx:is_passable(kx, ky) then
        ctx:move_units({h.id}, kx, ky)
    end
    ::continue::
end

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")
if #workers == 0 then return end

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do

    if deposit then
        ctx:gather({w.id}, deposit.id)
    end
end
```
Keep Hissers at 6 tiles distance, retreat further if too many enemies

-- Intent: kite
-- Description: Maintain range, retreat if outnumbered

local hissers = ctx:my_units("Hisser")
if #hissers == 0 then return end

for _, h in ipairs(hissers) do
    local nearby = ctx:enemies_in_range(h.x, h.y, 10)
    local enemy = ctx:nearest_enemy(h.x, h.y)
    if not enemy then goto continue end

    -- If outnumbered, retreat further
    local dist = 6
    if #nearby > 3 then dist = 10 end

    local kx, ky = ctx:position_at_range(h.x, h.y, enemy.x, enemy.y, dist)
    if kx then
        ctx:move_units({h.id}, kx, ky)
    end
    ::continue::
end

```lua
-- Intent: gather
-- Description: Send idle workers to nearest fish pond

local workers = ctx:idle_units("Pawdler")

for _, w in ipairs(workers) do
```

Hissers: maintain 6 tile range, focus weakest, retreat if below half HP

-- Intent: kite
-- Description: Multi-step Hisser positioning with focus fire and retreat

local hissers = ctx:my_units("Hisser")
if #hissers == 0 then return end

local healthy_ids = {}
for _, h in ipairs(hissers) do
    if ctx:hp_pct(h.id) < 0.5 then
        -- Retreat wounded
        local safe = ctx:safe_positions(h.id, 10)
        if #safe > 0 then
            ctx:move_units({h.id}, safe[1].x, safe[1].y)
        end
    else
        -- Maintain range
        local enemy = ctx:nearest_enemy(h.x, h.y)
        if enemy then
            local kx, ky = ctx:position_at_range(h.x, h.y, enemy.x, enemy.y, 6)
            if kx then
                ctx:move_units({h.id}, kx, ky)
            end
        end
        table.insert(healthy_ids, h.id)
    end
end

-- Focus fire with healthy Hissers
if #healthy_ids > 0 then
    ctx.behaviors:focus_weakest(healthy_ids, 8)
end
